label: Total Killed
contestNote: Does not include 19 hijackers. NYC ME records 2,753 at WTC; DoD records 184 at Pentagon; 40 on Flight 93.

label: First Responders Killed
contestNote: Includes 343 FDNY firefighters/paramedics, 37 Port Authority Police, 23 NYPD, 8 EMTs/others — largest single-incident loss of first responders in U.S. history.

label: Victim Compensation Fund Total Paid
source: September 11th Victim Compensation Fund Annual Report 2025
contestNote: Over 52,000 claims approved for first responders, survivors, and family members under the Zadroga Act.
deltaNote: Fund reauthorized permanently through 2092 in July 2019

label: Estimated Cost of U.S. War on Terror
source: Watson Institute, Brown University — Costs of War Project 2021
contestNote: Watson Institute estimates include direct military spending, care for veterans, homeland security, and interest on war debt. DOD estimates are lower, focusing only on direct military appropriations.

label: WTC Health Program Enrollment
source: NIOSH WTC Health Program Annual Report 2024
contestNote: Includes first responders, recovery workers, and Lower Manhattan residents/workers/students exposed to toxic dust. Over 4,000 9/11-related cancers certified.

label: U.S. Service Members Killed in Afghanistan
source: Department of Defense Personnel and Readiness
contestNote: Total U.S. military deaths in Operation Enduring Freedom and Resolute Support missions, 2001–2021.

label: 9/11 Insurance Claims Paid
contestNote: Largest insured loss in U.S. history at the time. Includes property damage, workers' compensation, life insurance, and aviation liability.
